message: Guy has already been released. Shouldn't you be able to meet him at his home?
Maybe it's because it's the off-season, or perhaps there just aren't that many people with business at the office, but there are no other guests besides me.
There are just a few small booths for business talks and the reception desk right in front of me, nothing particularly special.
I prefer the blonde girl hiding in the shadow.
When she sees me, she makes a blatant grimace. I don't want to see her either.
A charming face and a comically round body. She might appeal to the media and the public, but I won't be fooled by such looks.
It seems she has no intention of dropping her vague attitude no matter what happens. Does she think she can just ignore me?
You're in the wrong place to meet Guy.
I came to see you today.
Honestly, I’d prefer not to see you again.
Looks like I’m going to be busy again from now on.
Guy should be at home. Ask him about his matters.
More importantly, I heard gossip that the articles were put out by the team; is that true?
I don't want to create any weird misunderstandings. Listen, that article was something Guy had Eva write.
If you think it's a lie, go ask him directly.
You mean Gai would stoop to spreading gossip? I can't believe he would do something to ruin his own reputation.
So you were also involved in Gai's confinement, huh? You'll go as far as becoming a criminal for the baseball team, huh? That's some guts.
Please don't come back again. This time, I won't let you through this door.
